all.en: Sarah straightened her blue graduation cap and looked around the crowded auditorium.
all.zh: 莎拉整理好她的藍色畢業帽，環顧擁擠的禮堂。

all.en: Three years of high school had flown by faster than she had ever imagined possible.
all.zh: 三年的高中時光比她想像中還要飛快地過去了。

all.en: She remembered the shy girl she had been on her very first day of ninth grade.
all.zh: 她想起九年級第一天那個害羞的自己。

all.en: Back then, she had been afraid to raise her hand or speak in front of the class.
all.zh: 當時，她害怕舉手或在全班面前發言。

all.en: Now she was about to deliver a speech as the elected student representative.
all.zh: 如今她即將以民選學生代表的身分發表演說。

all.en: Her best friends sat in the row behind her, holding flowers and cheering quietly.
all.zh: 她最好的朋友們坐在她後面那一排，手裡拿著花，輕聲歡呼。

all.en: When her name was called, she walked to the stage with tears in her eyes.
all.zh: 當她的名字被喊到時，她眼中含著淚水走向舞台。

all.en: She spoke about friendship, hard work, and the teachers who had shaped her dreams.
all.zh: 她談到了友誼、努力，以及那些塑造了她夢想的老師們。

all.en: The audience applauded warmly, and her parents wiped tears from their proud faces.
all.zh: 觀眾熱烈鼓掌，她的父母從驕傲的臉上擦去淚水。

all.en: After the ceremony, classmates exchanged hugs and promised to stay in touch forever.
all.zh: 典禮結束後，同學們互相擁抱，承諾要永遠保持聯絡。

all.en: Sarah held her diploma tightly, ready to begin the next chapter of her life.
all.zh: 莎拉緊握著她的畢業證書，準備好開始她人生的下一個篇章。

all.en: Outside the auditorium, Sarah's parents pulled her into a long and emotional embrace.
all.zh: 在禮堂外，莎拉的父母把她拉進一個漫長而動情的擁抱裡。

all.en: Her younger brother teased her about crying, but his eyes were also a little wet.
all.zh: 她的弟弟取笑她哭得太誇張，但他自己的眼眶也微微泛紅。

all.en: The group of best friends gathered under the old oak tree for a final photo.
all.zh: 那群最要好的朋友聚集在老橡樹下，拍下最後一張合照。

all.en: They promised to meet for coffee at least once every two months no matter what.
all.zh: 她們承諾無論如何每兩個月至少要見一次面喝咖啡。

all.en: Sarah's homeroom teacher walked over and gave her a small leather-bound notebook.
all.zh: 莎拉的導師走過來，送給她一本小小的皮面筆記本。

all.en: Inside the cover was a short message wishing her courage on every future road.
all.zh: 封面內頁寫著一段簡短的話，祝福她在未來的每條路上都充滿勇氣。

all.en: That evening, the family celebrated with a quiet dinner at her favorite Italian restaurant.
all.zh: 當晚，全家在她最愛的義式餐廳安靜地用餐慶祝。

all.en: Her father proposed a toast, calling her the bravest young woman he had ever known.
all.zh: 父親舉杯敬酒，稱她為他認識最勇敢的年輕女子。

all.en: Back in her room, Sarah pinned the diploma above her desk for daily inspiration.
all.zh: 回到房間後，莎拉把畢業證書釘在書桌上方，作為每日的激勵。

all.en: Before bed, she opened her acceptance letter and quietly began packing for university.
all.zh: 睡前，她翻開錄取通知，靜靜地開始為大學生活打包行李。

all.en: Two days later, Sarah loaded her suitcases into the back of the family car.
all.zh: 兩天後，莎拉把行李箱裝進家裡轎車的後車廂。

all.en: Her brother insisted on coming along to help her move into the dormitory.
all.zh: 她的弟弟堅持要一起去，幫她搬進大學宿舍。

all.en: The drive took five hours, filled with old songs and quiet conversations.
all.zh: 車程花了五個小時，沿途充滿老歌和安靜的對話。

all.en: Her new roommate greeted her with a homemade banner taped above the bed.
all.zh: 她的新室友以一條手作橫幅迎接她，貼在床鋪上方。

all.en: Sarah unpacked carefully, placing the leather notebook on her bedside table.
all.zh: 莎拉小心翼翼地拆行李，把那本皮面筆記本放在床頭櫃上。

all.en: That night, she wrote her first journal entry about leaving home for good.
all.zh: 那天晚上，她寫下了第一篇日記，記錄真正離家的心情。

all.en: Classes started the next morning, and she signed up for a creative writing course.
all.zh: 課程隔天早上開始，她報名了一門創意寫作課。

all.en: She joined the campus volunteer club, hoping to meet kindhearted new friends.
all.zh: 她加入了校園志工社，希望結識心地善良的新朋友。

all.en: Every Sunday, she called her parents to share funny stories from the week.
all.zh: 每個星期天，她都會打電話給父母，分享一週的趣事。

all.en: Slowly, the unfamiliar campus began to feel like a second home to her.
all.zh: 漸漸地，這座陌生的校園開始讓她感覺像第二個家。

all.en: By the second semester, Sarah was confident enough to lead a student writing workshop.
all.zh: 到了第二學期，莎拉已自信到能主持一場學生寫作工作坊。

all.en: She invited classmates to share short pieces about their hometowns and family memories.
all.zh: 她邀請同學分享關於家鄉和家庭回憶的短篇文章。

all.en: The leather notebook from her homeroom teacher slowly filled with new ideas and sketches.
all.zh: 那本來自導師的皮面筆記本，慢慢被新點子和草圖填滿。

all.en: One afternoon, her writing professor praised her honest voice in front of the whole class.
all.zh: 一個下午，她的寫作教授當著全班的面稱讚了她真誠的筆觸。

all.en: Sarah blushed but felt the same warmth she had felt on her graduation stage.
all.zh: 莎拉臉紅了，卻感受到和畢業典禮舞台上一樣的溫暖。

all.en: During winter break, she returned home with a small notebook of finished short stories.
all.zh: 寒假時，她帶著一本寫滿短篇故事的小筆記本回家。

all.en: Her younger brother demanded to read every story before anyone else got the chance.
all.zh: 她的弟弟要求在任何人之前搶先閱讀每一篇故事。

all.en: He laughed at one tale and quietly wiped his eyes at another by the fireplace.
all.zh: 他對其中一則故事笑出聲，又在壁爐邊安靜地擦了擦另一則的眼淚。

all.en: Sarah mailed a copy of her favorite story to her old high school homeroom teacher.
all.zh: 莎拉把自己最喜愛的一篇故事寄了一份給高中時的導師。

all.en: A week later, a letter arrived saying her honest voice had only grown stronger.
all.zh: 一週後，一封回信寄達，說她真誠的聲音只變得更加堅定有力。

all.en: By her second year, Sarah began submitting short stories to local literary magazines.
all.zh: 進入大二時，莎拉開始把短篇故事投稿給當地的文學雜誌。

all.en: Her first published piece described the old oak tree from her graduation photograph.
all.zh: 她第一篇發表的作品描寫的是畢業合照中那棵老橡樹。

all.en: The editor mailed her a small check and an invitation to a regional writers' retreat.
all.zh: 編輯寄給她一張小額支票，並邀請她參加區域性的作家靜修營。

all.en: Sarah attended the retreat and met published authors who had once been just like her.
all.zh: 莎拉參加了靜修營，認識了一些曾經和她一樣起步的已出版作家。

all.en: They encouraged her to keep writing honestly about the people who shaped her childhood.
all.zh: 他們鼓勵她繼續真誠地書寫那些塑造她童年的人物。

all.en: On her twenty-first birthday, her parents framed her first published essay as a gift.
all.zh: 在她二十一歲生日當天，父母把她第一篇發表的散文裱框送她當作禮物。

all.en: Her brother surprised her with a leather pen engraved with the date of her graduation.
all.zh: 弟弟送了她一支皮製鋼筆當作驚喜，筆身上刻著她畢業的日期。

all.en: Sarah began collecting her stories into a small manuscript for a graduation memoir.
all.zh: 莎拉開始把自己的故事集結成一份小手稿，準備寫成一本畢業回憶錄。

all.en: She dedicated the opening page to the homeroom teacher who had believed in her voice.
all.zh: 她把扉頁獻給那位曾相信她聲音的導師。

all.en: On publication day, she returned to the auditorium where her journey had truly begun.
all.zh: 新書出版那天，她回到了那座開啟自己旅程的禮堂。
